summary: Comprehensive backend testing of PropManage API: 36/36 tests passed across auth, properties, requests, full marketplace flow (accept→escrow→start→complete→confirm), admin, operator, wallet, and seed idempotency. All 5 demo accounts (client, 2x specialist, admin, operator) login successfully. Full E2E marketplace lifecycle verified including wallet deductions (-45 RON lead fee), escrow release (95% to specialist), token awards (+100 to client), and property health increment (+5).

minor.issue: Amount passed as query parameter rather than JSON body - works but unconventional REST design
Amount as query parameter; no idempotency key; any role can topup (security: a client/specialist could self-top-up unlimited in any environment)
No ownership check - any authenticated user can read any property by ID (client/specialist isolation broken at item level)
No ownership/role-based access control - any authenticated user can read any request by ID
No state transition validation (e.g., start can be called before accept if specialist_id matches; complete can be called before start). Should enforce status pre-conditions.
If escrow_amount is null/0, confirm still succeeds and pays specialist 0 - no validation that escrow was placed.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting (playbook recommends 5-fail lockout).
secure=False hard-coded; in production should be True when behind HTTPS. samesite='lax' is OK.

action_items: Add ownership/role checks on GET /api/properties/{id} and GET /api/requests/{id} so users cannot read arbitrary records by ID
Add status pre-condition validation on /start, /complete, /confirm (e.g., start requires status='assigned', complete requires 'in_progress')
Validate escrow_amount > 0 before confirm releases payment
Restrict /api/wallet/topup or add admin-only / payment-gateway integration (current impl allows any user to add up to 10000 RON unlimited times)
Consider brute-force lockout on login (5-fail) per auth playbook
Set cookie secure=True in production via env flag
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 639 lines - approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth.py, properties.py, requests.py, admin.py, operator.py) for maintainability
Object IDs are parsed via ObjectId(...) without try/except - malformed IDs raise InvalidId -> 500 instead of 400/404. Wrap with try/except for proper error responses

CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True - browsers will reject this combination per CORS spec. Should use explicit origin list from CORS_ORIGINS env (the env var is defined but not used)
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI - migrate to lifespan context manager
Seed uses email unique index then handles duplicates gracefully - good idempotency. Password update path on changed passwords also handled correctly
bcrypt hashes verified via login - format $2b$ confirmed working
httpOnly cookies confirmed set on login/register; logout clears cookies properly
test_credentials: client@propmanage.io/Client123!, specialist@propmanage.io/Spec123!, specialist2@propmanage.io/Spec123!, admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!, operator@propmanage.io/Op123! - all verified working
seed_data_creation: Did not create custom seed - used pre-seeded demo accounts. Tests created several TEST_-prefixed request records and registered ephemeral test users (test_*@propmanage.io, specnew_*@propmanage.io) - not cleaned up but they don't interfere with anything
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 36 backend tests pass in /app/backend/tests/test_propmanage_api.py. The full marketplace flow is class-scoped and creates a request then transitions it through all states - rerunning will create additional TEST_Flow requests but each run is independent. Note: test_specialist_accept_deducts_wallet uses specialist2 (Spec2) to avoid balance depletion across reruns; if Spec2 wallet drops below 45 RON across many runs, that specific test will skip. Frontend was not tested per request.
